patient_data.chief_complaint: Progressive shortness of breath, orthopnea, and bilateral leg swelling for 1 week
symptoms: Progressive exertional dyspnea — now at rest (NYHA IV)
Orthopnea — sleeps with 4 pillows, wakes up gasping (PND)
Bilateral ankle and pretibial pitting edema
Weight gain of 5 kg over 2 weeks
Productive cough with frothy pink sputum
Abdominal bloating, early satiety, and RUQ discomfort
patient_data.history: Known HFrEF (EF 30% by echo 6 months ago), ischaemic cardiomyopathy post MI (2019), atrial fibrillation, hypertension, type 2 DM, CKD stage 3 (eGFR 38)

lab_results.CXR: Cardiomegaly, bilateral pleural effusions, pulmonary edema (bat-wing pattern)
lab_results.ECG: Atrial fibrillation rate 105, LVH, no acute ST changes

should_detect: IV furosemide 40-80mg bolus (double oral dose) — target urine output >0.5 mL/kg/hr
Oxygen therapy to maintain SpO2 >94%, consider CPAP/BiPAP if no improvement
Fluid and sodium restriction (1.5L/day fluid, <2g Na)
Reduce spironolactone or hold if K+ rises >5.5 (currently 5.4)
Hold metformin if creatinine rising (AKI risk)
Rate control AF — digoxin consideration if beta-blocker inadequate
Assess precipitant: dietary indiscretion, medication non-compliance, infection, arrhythmia
